title: COVID-19 summaries of recent evidence - Canada.ca
content:
## COVID-19 summaries of recent evidence
The Public Health Agency of Canada continually reviews the rapidly emerging evidence on COVID-19 to facilitate integration of the most current high quality information into our decision-making, guidance and recommendations.
To help decision makers respond to COVID-19, we produce rapid technical reviews that identify, categorize and briefly summarize the scientific literature to answer priority questions about COVID-19. These evidence reviews are identified below.
Web accessible versions of the reviews are being developed and will be available below as they are completed.
To request a copy of a review not yet available online, email us at ocsoevidence-bcscdonneesprobantes@phac-aspc.gc.ca .
Additional evidence from public health partners we coordinate with can be found online at the National Collaborating Centre for Methods and Tools (NCCMT) , and the COVID-19 Evidence Network to support Decision-making (COVID-END) .

## SARS-CoV-2 transmission

- November 25, 2021: Evidence brief on the risk of COVID-19 transmission in flight, update 3

- May 4, 2021: Rapid review on the impact of school closures and re-openings on COVID-19 transmission

- January 20, 2021: Evidence brief on adherence to isolation and quarantine
